fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form (Document 1) listed ICD-10 code M54.5 (Low back pain) as the primary diagnosis. However, both the clinical notes (Document 2) and the physician prescription (Document 3) specify M47.816 (Spondylosis with radiculopathy, lumbar region) as the correct primary diagnosis, supported by MRI findings dated 01/15/2026. The clinical notes explicitly state the primary diagnosis was corrected per MRI and demote M54.5 to a secondary code. M47.816 from the clinical notes and prescription has been adopted as the authoritative primary ICD-10 code.
fields.priority_reason: Patient requires a bariatric power mobility device due to severe functional limitation (unable to ambulate more than 15 feet), MRI-confirmed lumbar spondylosis with bilateral radiculopathy, and an ICD code conflict that was resolved with high confidence but warrants clinical review.
fields.notes: 1) Authorization Ref # is blank on the referral form — prior authorization from Pacific Mutual Workers Comp should be obtained before fulfillment. 2) Bariatric model with minimum 400 lb weight capacity is required; standard K0823 units are contraindicated for this patient. 3) Vietnamese interpreter must be arranged for all appointments including delivery. 4) ICD conflict resolved: M54.5 demoted to secondary per physician's clinical notes and prescription. 5) Policy Number: PM-WC-2026-441892. 6) Preferred appointment window and transportation fields were left blank on the referral form and should be confirmed with the adjuster or patient.

email_body:
Dear Linda Torres,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding an active durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your insured, **Michelle Tran**, under claim number **WC-2026-086210**.

In reviewing the referral documentation, we have identified the following items that are required to move forward with processing:

1. **Authorization Reference Number (auth_ref)** *(Hard Block – routing on hold)*
2. **Appointment Window (appt_window)** *(Hard Block – routing on hold)*
3. **Transportation Arrangements (transportation)*

Please note that items 1 and 2 are currently preventing us from routing this referral. Processing will remain on hold until both of these fields are resolved.

We kindly ask that you provide the outstanding information **within 24 business hours** of receiving this email to avoid any further delays in care for Ms. Tran. You may reply directly to this message or contact our intake team at your earliest convenience.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. We look forward to your response.

Warm regards,

**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME | Intake Department
